app.name: jsonargparse
app.one_liner: Minimal effort CLIs derived from type hints and parse from command line, config files and environment variables

screens.name: CLI Configuration Interface
screens.purpose: Allow users to define and run configurable Python applications via command-line interfaces generated from type-annotated functions or classes.
primary_actions: Generate CLI from typed function using auto_cli()
Parse and merge arguments from CLI, config files, and environment variables

rust_commands.name: add_class_arguments
rust_commands.purpose: Register all parameters of a class constructor as CLI arguments, enabling dependency injection and configuration.

rust_commands.name: add_function_arguments
rust_commands.purpose: Register all parameters of a given function as CLI arguments using type hints and docstrings.

rust_commands.name: auto_cli
rust_commands.purpose: Automatically generate and run a command-line interface from a type-hinted Python function with zero boilerplate.

rust_commands.name: instantiate_classes
rust_commands.purpose: Instantiate classes based on parsed configuration, supporting nested structures and dependency resolution.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add class and function argument registration
week 1: Enable auto_cli() for minimal-function CLI generation
week 1: Implement core ArgumentParser extension with action='config'
week 1: Support Union and Literal types in argument parsing
acceptance_tests: A user can define a function with type-hinted parameters and run it via auto_cli() without writing any argument-parsing code.
Classes registered via add_class_arguments() are instantiated with correct dependencies after parse_args().
Complex types like Union[int, Literal['off']] are parsed and validated correctly from CLI input.
The parser correctly merges values from command-line arguments, a YAML config file, and environment variables according to precedence rules.
